sections.paragraph:
πλέκω 1 aor. ἔπλεξα. Pass.: aor. ptc. πλακείς Is 28:5; pf. ptc. πεπλεγμένος (Hom. et al.; POsl 159, 10; 19 [III a.d.]; LXX; TestSol; JosAs ch. 11 cod. A [p. 53, ln. 11 Bat.]; ApcrEzk [Epiph. 70, 10]; EpArist 70; Philo, Aet. M. 105; Jos., Ant. 3, 170) weave, plait στέφανον a wreath (Epici p. 23, Cypria Fgm. 4, 2 K.=fgm 5 p. 47 B.; Pind. et al.) Mk 15:17. τὶ ἔκ τινος (Alciphron 2, 35, 1; Paus. 2, 35, 5 στεφ. ἐκ) Mt 27:29; J 19:2; pass. ApcPt 3:10.—B. 622. DELG. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pf. = perfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

  1. ch. = chapter(s)
  2. ln. = line

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Epiph Epiph , IV–V a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Epici Epici = fragments of Greek epic, various editions; V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Paus Paus , perieg., II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 824
